item.title: Wildlife management breached for years
item.description: The killing of a seven-year-old girl by the resident crocodile in a pond at Bagerhat’s Hazrat Khan Jahan Ali shrine on Monday night, following a similar attack on a dog in April, has exposed longstanding failures in wildlife management and public safety....

item.title: National Editors’ Council launched
item.description: A new organisation of newspaper editors, National Editors Council, was launched on Tuesday with Jaijaidin editor Shafik Rehman as convener and Amar Desh editor Mahmudur Rahman as joint convener....

item.title: RCC administrator, engineer seek govt nod for France trip to learn street-lighting tech
item.description: Rajshahi City Corporation administrator Mahfuzur Rahman Riton and its executive engineer (electricity), ABM Asaduzzaman Sweet, have sought government approval to visit France to gain knowledge of energy-efficient street-lighting technology....

item.title: DU prepares for 105th founding day celebrations
item.description: Dhaka University will celebrate Dhaka University Day on July 1 with a series of programmes marking the 105th year of the institution’s academic journey....

item.title: Bangladesh braces for hotter June
item.description: Rainfall across Bangladesh is likely to remain below normal in June while both day and night temperatures are expected to stay above average, according to the long-range weather forecast prepared by the Bangladesh Meteorological Department....

item.title: UIU rover team ranks 3rd globally at URC 2026
item.description: The UIU Mars Rover Team of United International University has secured third place globally and first place in Asia at the University Rover Challenge 2026, marking a historic achievement for Bangladesh and the region....

item.title: You’d be in prison if not for me: Trump blasts Netanyahu
item.description: United States president Donald Trump lashed out at Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu over the latter’s escalation in Lebanon in an expletive-laden phone call on Monday, according to American news outlet Axios, reports DAWN.com....

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah trade blows
item.description: Israel continued to strike southern Lebanon on Tuesday as Iran-backed Hezbollah attacked its troops there despite an apparent Washington-brokered de-escalation deal and a fourth round of US-hosted talks between Lebanon and Israel....

item.title: Dipen Dewan’s resignation sparks speculations
item.description: The sudden resignation of Chittagong Hill Tracts affairs minister Dipen Dewan has triggered speculation in political circles, with Bangladesh Nationalist Party leaders hinting at internal disagreements, ideological differences, and disputes over decision-making within the ministry....

item.title: AD-DIN NEWBORN DEATHS PROBE: Doubt deepens as post-mortem skipped
item.description: The investigation into the deaths of six newborns at Ad-Din Medical College Hospital in the capital’s Moghbazar area became uncertain as the authorities skipped autopsies of the newborns, potentially losing vital forensic evidence, said legal and medical experts....

item.title: Legal reforms alone can’t guarantee worker rights
item.description: BANGLADESH moving out of the world’s 10 worst countries for worker rights in the 2026 Global Rights Index of the International Trade Union Confederation for the first time after 2017 is a positive development. Bangladesh now has Rating 5, which means ‘no guarantee of rights,’ from having had Rating 5+, which means ‘no guarantee of rights due to the breakdown of the......

item.title: Govt should double up efforts to protect wildlife
item.description: FIGHT against wildlife crimes has slowed as a wildlife crime control unit report shows that it carried out no drives against poaching and wildlife smuggling in the first 23 days of May. The slowdown has followed a steady decline in enforcement over recent months. After 37 drives in January, 25 in February and 22 in March, the number of drives dropped to 12 in April, the......

item.title: Power cuts, road chaos mar rural Eid
item.description: EID-UL-AZHA was supposed to be a time of festive reunion. For a large majority in Bangladesh, it was a time of real suffering, discomfort, and frustration — long hours of power outages, delays in highways, and so on. Public suffering shows that the people in charge of transportation and electricity were a failure in their responsibilities. What was supposed to be an......

item.title: Independent inquiry needed
item.description: MORE than 570 children have died reportedly from measles-related illness. Behind every number is a child whose life ended too soon, a mother left with empty arms, a father carrying an unbearable grief and a family permanently altered. For these families, this is not a debate about policy, programmes, or budgets. It is a personal tragedy......

item.title: Bangladesh’s pursuit of strategic autonomy
item.description: LEADING English-language Ukrainian military news outlet Militarnyi in May reported that Bangladesh and the United States were in final stages of signing strategic defence agreements granting US military access to Bangladeshi ports and airfields. Citing the US Trade representative’s visit to Dhaka in early May, the story noted potential outcome although local media did......

item.title: What to eat? Who to complain to?
item.description: THERE is a particular kind of arithmetic that has begun to define everyday life. It is not taught in schools or explained in policy papers. It is done quietly at kitchen tables, in shared flats, at market stalls and on the way home from work: subtracting one rising cost from another, deciding what to leave out so the month does not collapse too early......

item.title: Van driver arrested for allegedly raping 7-year-old girl at Hatibandha
item.description: The police have arrested a battery-powered van driver Abu Taleb, 60, on charges of raping a seven-year-old child at Hatibandha in Lalmonirhat....

item.title: JU, JUCSU urge home minister to arrest attempted rape accused
item.description: The administration of Jahangirnagar University and the Jahangirnagar University Central Students Union on Tuesday urged home minister Salahuddin Ahmed to ensure the swift arrest of a man accused in the attempted rape of a female student on the campus....

item.title: Mymensingh gets new commissioner, Magura new DC
item.description: The government has appointed Health Services Division additional secretary SM Humayun Kabir Sarkar as the new divisional commissioner of Mymensingh and Ministry of Disaster Management and Relief deputy secretary Motakabbir Ahmed as the new deputy commissioner of Magura....

item.title: Eight killed in road accidents in Faridpur, Chattogram
item.description: At least eight people were killed and five more were injured in road accidents in Faridpur and Chattogram on Tuesday....

item.title: Prothom Alo photojournalist, brother attacked
item.description: Chief photographer of daily Prothom Alo’s feature department Kabir Hossain, 46, sustained serious injuries as miscreants attacked him with sharp weapons on Tuesday at Sirajdikhan upazila in Munshiganj....

item.title: Motorcyclist jailed for concealing number plate to evade AI cameras
item.description: A motorcyclist was jailed and fined on Tuesday for covering part of his motorcycle’s number plate to evade detection by the newly introduced AI-powered traffic surveillance cameras in Dhaka....

item.title: 20 held with 1,700 bags of cement bound for Myanmar
item.description: Bangladesh Navy on Tuesday seized two engine-powered boats carrying 1,700 bags of cement allegedly being smuggled to Myanmar and arrested 20 people in the Bay of Bengal near St Martins Island....

item.title: ONLINE GAMBLING, MONEY LAUNDERING: Govt prepares bill for new law
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed said on Tuesday that the government was preparing a new law to replace the colonial-era Gambling Act of 1867, focusing on tackling online gambling, betting and associated money laundering....

item.title: BERC likely to announce new power tariffs today
item.description: The Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission said on Tuesday that wholesale, transmission and retail electricity tariffs would be announced today....

item.title: 174 more Bangladeshis repatriated from Libya
item.description: One hundred and seventy four more Bangladeshi migrants were repatriated from Libya on Tuesday through a joint initiative of the Bangladesh government, Libyan authorities and the International Organization for Migration....

item.title: Storm injures 15 in Japan, prompts evacuation calls
item.description: A severe tropical storm battered southwestern Japan on Tuesday, injuring 15 people and prompting calls to evacuate for hundreds of thousands of people due to flooding and landslide risks....

item.title: Two lynched in C’nawabganj, Meherpur over theft suspicion
item.description: Two people were lynched by angry mobs in separate incidents in Chapainawabganj and Meherpur districts over theft suspicion early Tuesday....

item.title: Family card holder selection to be error-free, unbiased: minister
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Tuesday said that the selection of family card holders would be almost error-free....

item.title: JU tops Bangladesh in chemistry research ranking
item.description: Jahangirnagar University has ranked first among Bangladeshi universities and research institutions in chemistry in the latest Nature Index Ranking, highlighting its growing footprint in global scientific research....

item.title: Youth hacked to death in Khulna
item.description: A young man was hacked to death by unidentified assailants after being called out of his home in Khulna city on Tuesday....

item.title: ‘Toxic liquor’ kills 4 in Rangpur
item.description: The death toll from drinking ‘toxic liquor’ in Mithapukur upazila of Rangpur rose to four with the death of another man....

item.title: 12 injured in Chapainawabganj clash over land dispute
item.description: At least 12 people were injured in a clash between two rival groups over a land dispute at Shibganj upazila in Chapainawabganj on Tuesday....

item.title: BRTA warns over fake messages demanding online traffic fines
item.description: The Bangladesh Road Transport Authority in a public message has warned that many people were receiving fake messages in its name asking for payment of non-existent fines through fraudulent website links....

item.title: 7 held over vandalising BNP office, houses in Munshiganj
item.description: The police have arrested seven people in connection with a clash and vandalism centring on local dominance in the remote Char Balaki village of Gazaria upazila in Munshiganj....
